summary: The IDF killed two Islamic Jihad militants in Gaza and struck Hamas police infrastructure in Khan Younis. Drone threats were reported over Eilat and the northern border, with an Iran‑backed Houthi drone intercepted over Eilat. Iran cancelled all civil flights nationwide and an Israeli citizen was arrested for spying for Iranian intelligence.
key_points: Two Islamic Jihad militants were eliminated by the IDF in Gaza as they planned imminent attacks (Source: @inon_yttach/8790).
An Israeli citizen was detained for conducting security missions for Iranian intelligence, including photographing sensitive areas during the "Harry’s Whistle" war (Source: @New_security8200/201481).
Iran’s civil aviation authority issued a NOTAM closing western airspace, cancelling all flights across the country until further notice (Source: @kianmeli1/87810).
The IDF intercepted a Houthi‑launched drone over Eilat, preventing an attack on the southernmost Israeli city (Source: @manniefabian/58519).
Sirens warned of a suspected drone attack in Eilat and of rocket fire from Lebanon striking Kiryat Shmona and surrounding towns (Source: @manniefabian/58518; @manniefabian/58509).
IDF forces attacked Hamas’s main police headquarters in Khan Younis, targeting infrastructure used for planning terror operations (Source: @idf_telegram/24248).
Prime Minister Netanyahu warned that Iran and Hezbollah attempted to impose a new, unacceptable equation and pledged a strong response to any future attacks (Source: @almogboker78/17431).

sections.executive_summary: The IDF eliminated two Islamic Jihad militants in Gaza and struck Hamas police headquarters in Khan Younis. Iran halted all civil flights nationwide and an Israeli citizen was arrested for spying for Tehran. Drone threats over Eilat and rocket fire from Lebanon heightened alert levels across Israel.

points: Iran’s civil aviation authority issued a NOTAM closing the western airspace, resulting in the cancellation of all flights across the country until further notice, affecting both civilian and military movements (Source: @kianmeli1/87810).

An Israeli citizen was arrested for conducting security missions for Iranian intelligence, including photographing sensitive locations during the "Harry’s Whistle" conflict, indicating active Iranian espionage operations within Israel (Source: @New_security8200/201481).

Prime Minister Netanyahu stated that Iran and Hezbollah attempted to impose a new equation on Israel, which he rejected, and warned of a powerful response to any future Iranian attacks (Source: @almogboker78/17431).

Shin_persian reported that Iran and Hezbollah are weaker than before, but the struggle continues, and fire on the Iranian front is currently halted, suggesting a temporary de‑escalation (Source: @shin_persian).
The IDF intercepted a Houthi‑launched drone over Eilat, preventing an attack on the southernmost Israeli city and demonstrating active air defense engagement (Source: @manniefabian/58519).
Sirens sounded in Eilat warning of a suspected drone attack and in the Western Galilee, indicating multiple aerial threat alerts across the country (Source: @manniefabian/58518).
Rocket fire from Lebanon was reported hitting Kiryat Shmona and surrounding towns, prompting civil defense alerts in northern Israel (Source: @manniefabian/58509).

IDF forces attacked Hamas’s main police headquarters in Khan Younis, targeting infrastructure used for planning terror operations, reflecting ongoing offensive operations in Gaza (Source: @idf_telegram/24248).
Two Islamic Jihad militants were killed by the IDF in Gaza as they prepared imminent terror attacks, showing continued Israeli counter‑terror operations in the Strip (Source: @inon_yttach/8790).
The IDF strike on Hamas police headquarters in Khan Younis targeted command facilities used to plan and execute terror operations against Israel (Source: @idf_telegram/24248).
Rocket fire from Lebanon struck Kiryat Shmona and surrounding towns without prior warning, indicating renewed cross‑border hostilities (Source: @manniefabian/58509).

Netanyahu warned that Iran and Hezbollah attempted to impose a new equation on Israel and pledged a strong response to any future attacks, reflecting heightened tension with Lebanon (Source: @almogboker78/17431).

key_developments: The IDF eliminated two Islamic Jihad militants in Gaza who were planning immediate attacks (Source: @inon_yttach/8790).
An Israeli citizen was arrested for conducting security missions for Iranian intelligence, including photographing sensitive areas during the "Harry’s Whistle" war (Source: @New_security8200/201481).
Iran cancelled all civil flights nationwide by issuing a NOTAM closing western airspace (Source: @kianmeli1/87810).
The IDF intercepted a Houthi‑launched drone over Eilat, preventing an attack on the city (Source: @manniefabian/58519).
Sirens warned of a suspected drone attack in Eilat and of rocket fire from Lebanon hitting Kiryat Shmona (Source: @manniefabian/58518; @manniefabian/58509).
IDF forces struck Hamas’s main police headquarters in Khan Younis, targeting terror‑planning infrastructure (Source: @idf_telegram/24248).

Prime Minister Netanyahu declared that Iran and Hezbollah attempted to impose an unacceptable new equation on Israel and vowed a powerful response to any future aggression (Source: @almogboker78/17431).
sections.threat_assessment: Iranian intelligence activities within Israel, evidenced by the arrest of a local spy, indicate a sustained covert threat, while the cancellation of civil flights disrupts regional mobility and may mask military logistics. Drone incursions from Houthi forces and rocket fire from Lebanon demonstrate a multi‑front aerial threat to Israeli territory. Hamas infrastructure remains a target, but its capability to launch attacks persists, as shown by the planned Islamic Jihad operation.
sections.regional_response: Israel has responded with heightened air defenses, strikes on Hamas command sites, and public warnings of strong retaliation against Iranian and Hezbollah aggression. Iranian authorities have limited civilian air traffic, potentially to consolidate control and prepare for further operations. Lebanese actors continue rocket launches, prompting Israeli alerts and diplomatic warnings from Netanyahu.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of Iranian espionage networks should be intensified, focusing on digital currency payments and surveillance missions. Drone detection assets need reinforcement along the southern and northern borders due to Houthi and Lebanese threats. Continued surveillance of Hamas and Islamic Jihad cells in Gaza is essential to preempt imminent attacks.
